evidence_excerpt: Cutaneous manifestations remained quiet and arthralgias improved, with sustained control on methotrexate, adalimumab, and minocycline for 3 years; however, ongoing treatment was required and symptoms did not clearly resolve completely.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Skin disease remained quiet and arthralgias improved on long-term therapy (3 years), indicating ongoing but mild residual symptoms managed with medications.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: After 12 weeks of etanercept plus methotrexate, skin lesions significantly improved and there was less joint pain, stiffness, and itching.
Six weeks later, etanercept was replaced with adalimumab while continuing methotrexate and prednisone.
Following adalimumab, the patient improved and was completely weaned off steroids over a 2-month period.
Due to persistent stiffness and arthralgias with synovitis, minocycline was added to the regimen.
On the combination of methotrexate, adalimumab, and minocycline, cutaneous manifestations remained quiet and arthralgias improved, maintained for the last 3 years.

notes_on_uncertainty: Long-term functional status is not explicitly described; authors state remission, but text notes improved (not fully resolved) arthralgias under continued therapy. Timing between adalimumab initiation and minocycline addition is approximate; events categorized conservatively based on relative intervals.
